claim: Perception of partner superiority, not objective encouragement, drives motivational benefit in conjunctive task performance.
ME/CFS shows convergent genetic disruption across immune, endocrine, and metabolic pathways rather than single-locus etiology.
Priming effects depend critically on whether targets process information unconsciously, resolvable through response-effort modulation.
Didactically-structured cognitive training improves decision and timing performance but not general marksmanship, showing narrow transfer.
Auditory and visual flankers differ in whether interference occurs at perceptual vs post-perceptual stages, indicating processing route interaction.
